title: arabis alpina subsp. lerchenfeldiana
intro: Arabis alpina subsp. lerchenfeldiana, also known as the Alpine Rockcress, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Arabis alpina subsp. lerchenfeldiana can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
